gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti with Max-Q Design
notes:
I've only played around an hour so far, but game installs and runs flawlessly without tweaks. I have one .net error popup when launching but it doesn't seem to affect anything.

Performance was fine, maybe about 70% of the FPS of Day of Infamy / Insurgency, which are native games using the same engine. Red Orchestra 2 maps are bigger and have far more players, though.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: Mouse pointer is't visible in Steam overlay (shift + tab). Sound goes crazy when someone is auto-firing, random crashing. Graohics quality and overall gameplay is the same if not better than windows.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: Works, performance looks good looking only at fps, but it really feels stuttery when running. Low settings did not change that. Playable, but it would be great if it would work properly.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: It doesn't run with native performance, but it's still playable. When you open it you get a punkbuster error, but it still works. Steam overlay works but the mouse is invisible. I would recommend it, but don't expect it to run perfect.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes:
Heroes of Stalingrad:
Performance is good, but far from native performance. All the AntiCheat works great except an error the first time you open the game. There are no graphical artifacts at all, and the game is very playable. I'd put it somewhere between silver and gold ...................................................................................................................................................

Rising Storm:
The game works, but with noticable worse performance HoS. Just like Stalingrad, the anti cheat works perfectly. There are a few graphical artifacts, that make building change color, depending on the distance. There are also some maps that turn black for no reason, which makes those maps UNPLAYABLE. The game is bronze IMO ...................................................................................................................................................

I only experienced 1 or 2 crashes while loading the map with ~22 hours on the GNU/Linux platform. Zero crashes midgame. I'll look forward to Rising Storm 2 becoming playable on GNU/Linux. I'll stay with Red Orchestra and Insurgency until then.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: Initial run it complains about .NET, but after a couple more attempts to run it it runs just fine. Both Red Orchestra and Rising Storm maps
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: There's a bit of stutter the first ~15 sec after joining a server, but except that it runs really good.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: The game worked out of the box on Proton, tested only training sessions. I did install additionally D9VK and it worked as well with it's DX9 to Vulkan translation.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: Game works and plays, including multiplayer matches. Performance doesn't match what I would get in Windows on this system, but it is still very playable.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

notes: It works out of the box, with some slight stuttering upon first loading a map and some screen tearing.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm

cpu: AMD Athlon 200GE with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: About 10FPS lower than Windows and has more stutters, it also rarely crashes and it'll try to install .Net Framework every time you launch it and it has a prompt that you must close. Aside from that, it runs fine.
title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad with Rising Storm
